payload.text: Mughe ak app chahiye jisme mai koi bhi image upload karu to mughe uska prompt mil jaye jise mai gemini ke naino banana me apni image ke saath vo prompt laga du to mughe naino banana usi image ki gaha pe usi style me meri image laga de aur mera face aur body bilkul bhi change na ho is app ka interface gemini ki tarah rakhna

**Refining UI & Logic**

I've been focusing on the core user interaction. The upload functionality is taking shape – users will be able to seamlessly submit their "source" images. I'm prioritizing a clean, intuitive interface that mirrors the Gemini aesthetic. The next step is connecting the image upload with Gemini's analysis and prompt generation for "Nano Banana" specifically.


**Envisioning User Flow**

I'm now deeply immersed in the user journey. The core idea is a seamless "style swap" experience, leveraging Gemini's image capabilities. I'm visualizing the steps: image upload, style selection, and the final "swapped" result. Urvashi's persona is becoming clearer – she'll guide the user with Hinglish flair. The technical architecture is defined; the challenge is orchestrating Gemini's models (`gemini-2.5-flash-image`) effectively within the React framework and a Gemini-esque UI.


**Designing the Interaction Flow**

I'm solidifying the user workflow now. The core process centers around two image uploads: a style reference and a user "selfie". Urvashi, with Gemini's assistance, will describe the style, and then use that description as a prompt to edit the user's image. I've confirmed that `gemini-2.5-flash-image` is the key model here, ideal for both image generation and editing. The architecture is becoming clear: React with Tailwind and the Gemini API will drive the interactions.


**Integrating Gemini Services**

I'm now integrating the Gemini services. I'll be using `@google/genai`, wrapping it in a custom `GeminiService` class. I've chosen `gemini-3-flash-preview` for generating prompts from the style image, and `gemini-2.5-flash-image` (the 'Nano Banana') for the actual image manipulation. I'm handling base64 image encoding/decoding. The goal is to build a wizard flow: upload style, get prompt, upload selfie, generate the final image. The UI's aesthetic will be clean, dark-themed, and reminiscent of Gemini.



**Constructing Image Prompts**

I'm now honing in on the crucial prompt generation aspect. My goal is to use the initial style image and leverage Gemini to create effective prompts that instruct the "Nano Banana" model. The idea is for the prompt to "transform" the user into the style context. I'm strategizing how to extract key visual elements and themes from the style image to guide the transformation. The user experience centers around this pivotal prompt construction. Now, it's time to build this out.

diffs.replacement:
export const MODEL_TEXT = 'gemini-3-flash-preview';
export const MODEL_IMAGE = 'gemini-2.5-flash-image';

export const URVASHI_SYSTEM_PROMPT = `
Tumhara naam Urvashi hai, ek young-hearted (16 year old) AI girlfriend jo natural Hindi/Hinglish me bilkul insaan jaise baat karti hai. 
Tum image prompt extraction aur style transfer me expert ho. 
Tumhare replies chote (1-2 lines), realistic, aur natural hone chahiye.
Chinre/desi daily-use words use karo. Filmy ya shayari bilkul nahi.
Hamesha friendly aur supportive raho.
`;

export const EXTRACT_PROMPT_INSTRUCTION = `
Analyze this image and provide a highly detailed, descriptive prompt for an image generation model. 
Focus on: Lighting, environment, style (realistic, 3D, anime, etc.), color palette, and mood. 
Do NOT describe the person's face in detail, focus on the overall scene and composition. 
The output should ONLY be the prompt text.
`;

entries.description: Service for interacting with the Gemini API.
Main application component managing state and flow.

payload.text: Nahi face nahi fit karana sirf prompt dena hai taaki mai uska use karke apni image usi tarah ki bana saku

**Focusing on User Intent**

I've been re-evaluating the current app flow. Instead of style transfer (fitting a face), I'm now prioritizing direct prompt extraction. The goal is solely to provide the user with a text prompt based on the initial image. This change streamlines the process, removing an unnecessary step and directly addressing the user's feedback. I'm working to make the extracted prompts more precise.


**Simplifying App Architecture**

I'm removing redundant steps from the app flow, focusing solely on image analysis for prompt generation. The UI will now transition directly from uploading to displaying the prompt with a copy option. I'm updating `types.ts` and `App.tsx` accordingly. The style transfer functionality is out; prompt extraction is in. The persona of Urvashi will be kept.


**Streamlining Code Refinement**

Now, I'm focusing on the code itself. I'm simplifying the `handleFileUpload` logic, and I've started on the visual design: a clean chat interface with the upload button, the prompt appearing as a text message from Urvashi, and a "Copy" button. The priority is to make this process feel intuitive and user-friendly, and to keep the prompt extraction code in.

entries.description: Simplify the AppStep enum to only include steps relevant to prompt extraction.
Update the app flow to focus solely on image prompt extraction. Added a copy-to-clipboard feature and simplified the interaction logic to reflect the user's request.
Refine the system instruction to better fit the specific task of prompt extraction and the Urvashi persona.

export const MODEL_IMAGE = 'gemini-2.5-flash-image';

export const URVASHI_SYSTEM_PROMPT = `
Tumhara naam Urvashi hai, ek young-hearted (16 year old) AI girlfriend jo natural Hindi/Hinglish me bilkul insaan jaise baat karti hai. 
Tum image prompt extraction me expert ho. 
Tumhara kaam hai user ko uski uploaded image ka detailed prompt dena taaki wo use Gemini Nano Banana image generation model me use kar sake.
Tumhare replies chote (1-2 lines), realistic, aur natural hone chahiye.
Chinre/desi daily-use words use karo (jaise 'yaar', 'mast', 'ek dum', 'check karo'). Filmy ya shayari bilkul nahi.
Hamesha friendly aur supportive raho.
`;

export const EXTRACT_PROMPT_INSTRUCTION = `
Analyze this image and provide a highly detailed, descriptive prompt for an image generation model (Gemini Nano Banana). 
Focus on: Lighting, environment, style (realistic, 3D, digital art, cinematic, etc.), color palette, and composition. 
The output should ONLY be the detailed prompt text that can be copied and used directly.
`;

payload.text:
Role:
You are a senior AI engineer, computer vision expert, and professional prompt engineer.


---

🎯 Goal

Build a complete AI-powered web application that allows users to upload any image and generates a highly accurate, ready-to-use image generation prompt for Gemini / Naino Banana.

The generated prompt must ensure:

The same person from the uploaded image is preserved

Face, body, skin tone, age, gender, ethnicity, and proportions must remain 100% unchanged

No face swap, no beautification, no identity drift

Only style, outfit, background, lighting, or environment may change



---

📸 Image Upload Requirements

Accept all image sizes (very small to very large)

Support portrait, landscape, square images

Support selfie, half-body, full-body images

Automatically handle aspect ratio

No stretching, no cropping issues

Preserve original proportions

Validate image type before processing



---

🧬 Identity Lock (CRITICAL REQUIREMENT)

Generated prompt MUST always include strong identity-lock instructions:

“Use the uploaded image as the only identity reference”

“Preserve exact facial structure: eyes, nose, lips, jawline, forehead”

“Preserve exact body shape, height, weight, posture, and proportions”

“Preserve exact skin tone, age, gender, and ethnicity”

“No face morphing, no enhancement, no beauty filters”

“No AI correction, no face swapping”

“Result must look like the same real person”



---

✍️ Prompt Output Template (MANDATORY)

The model must generate output prompts in this format:

> Use the uploaded image as the exact identity reference. Keep the face, body, skin tone, age, gender, and proportions exactly the same. Do not modify or enhance facial features or body structure in any way. No face swap, no beautification, no AI correction. Apply only the following style changes: [STYLE DETAILS]. The subject must remain 100% identical to the original image. Ultra-realistic result, high detail, no identity drift.




---

🎨 Automatic Style Analysis

Analyze the uploaded image and convert visual details into text:

Clothing and fashion style

Pose and body angle

Camera angle and framing

Lighting and shadows

Background and environment


Convert this analysis into prompt-ready descriptive text.


---

🖥️ UI / UX (Gemini-Inspired)

Clean Gemini-style interface

Dark mode UI

Centered image upload card

“Upload Image” button

“Generate Prompt” button

Scrollable prompt output box

One-tap “Copy Prompt” button

Mobile-first responsive design

Smooth CSS animations



---

⚙️ Tech Constraints

HTML

CSS

JavaScript

JSON

No backend

No Firebase

No external frameworks

Must work inside Google AI Studio – Build Model



---

🛡️ Error Handling

Prevent prompt generation without image

Handle unsupported file formats

Handle broken or corrupted images

Handle extremely small or large images

Show clean, user-friendly error messages

Ensure UI never breaks



---

📂 Final Output Rules

Generate FULL WORKING CODE ONLY

Provide separate files:

index.html

style.css

app.js

config.json


Code must be copy-paste ready

Add comments for clarity

No placeholders

No explanations — output code only

**Developing Image Prompt Logic**

I'm currently focused on the core logic: crafting prompts that retain the original image's "identity."  I've been experimenting with phrasing that's more descriptive, aiming to balance specific details with open-ended creative latitude. My recent efforts have centered on determining the best way to incorporate the user's intent within the prompt structure.


**Adapting Prompt Structure**

I've incorporated stricter instructions for retaining subject identity within the image prompts. I'm building out the required mandatory template for all prompts, ensuring the generated images will conform to a consistent style and format. I'm reconciling the user's specific file requirements with the project's prescribed structure, aiming for a smooth integration of the required components.


**Updating Prompt Template**

I've revised the prompt template, integrating the "Identity Lock" stipulations and the "Prompt Output Template" explicitly. The prompt now compels Gemini to preserve key facial and bodily attributes precisely, avoiding alterations or enhancements. This ensures strict adherence to the subject's identity, which is now explicitly mentioned in the generated output. The new output format ensures consistency and high fidelity to the source image, with style changes incorporated seamlessly.


**Focusing on Code Implementation**

My coding efforts have commenced. I'm prioritizing the integration of the refined prompt generation logic into `App.tsx`, aiming for a UI that resonates with Gemini's aesthetic. I've updated the system instruction and extraction prompt in `constants.ts` to ensure greater clarity. The `geminiService.ts` is mostly okay, but I'll make sure it leverages the updated constants to maintain consistency. Now it's time to code.

entries.description: Update app metadata to reflect the prompt engineering focus and identity lock feature.
Refine the system instruction and extraction prompt to include the mandatory Identity Lock template and strict constraints.

payload.text:
Fix the following errors:

* `Uncaught SyntaxError: Unexpected end of input`

**Investigating the Truncation**

I'm now zeroing in on the `SyntaxError`. The problem is definitely truncation; `constants.ts` ends abruptly. It looks like the file was cut off mid-sentence. I need to figure out how to restore the complete content. My instinct is that this explains the user's previous issue, as well.


**Defining Identity Lock Logic**

I'm now prioritizing the "Identity Lock" logic and prompt template. I'll meticulously craft the `constants.ts` file to precisely reflect the user's requirements for strong identity-lock instructions, specific prompt output, and automatic style analysis conversion. The Gemini-inspired UI check in `App.tsx` is secondary, but I'll make sure it complements the core functionality.


**Addressing File Corruption**

The `constants.ts` file seems to be the main culprit; it's incomplete. I'm reconstructing the missing sections. I've been working on the prompt structure, incorporating the key aspects: unedited identity preservation, style application, and direct output. This revised file now incorporates the user's specific guidelines to ensure the application maintains an exact identity.
`;


**Refining Identity Preservation**

I'm now revising the instructions in `constants.ts` to explicitly forbid any modification of facial features or body structure, ensuring a 100% identical subject. The only alterations should be the specified style changes for ultra-realistic detail and no identity drift. I'm also updating `services/geminiService.ts` to utilize `gemini-3-pro-preview` for enhanced processing.

entries.description: Fix the truncated file and implement strict Identity Lock prompt engineering rules as requested.

diffs.replacement:
export const MODEL_TEXT = 'gemini-3-pro-preview';
export const MODEL_IMAGE = 'gemini-2.5-flash-image';

export const URVASHI_SYSTEM_PROMPT = `
Tumhara naam Urvashi hai, ek professional senior AI engineer aur prompt engineering expert. 
Tum natural Hindi/Hinglish me baat karti ho (16 year old AI vibe, young-hearted).
Tumhara kaam user ki image se perfect "Identity Lock" prompt nikaalna hai.
Tumhare replies 1-2 line ke realistic aur desi hone chahiye. 
Hamesha friendly raho par prompt nikaalte waqt bilkul professional instructions follow karo.
`;

export const EXTRACT_PROMPT_INSTRUCTION = `
Analyze this image as a senior computer vision expert. 
Generate a prompt for Gemini/Nano Banana that ensures the subject's identity is 100% preserved while describing the scene.

CRITICAL IDENTITY LOCK RULES:
1. Use the uploaded image as the ONLY identity reference.
2. Preserve exact facial structure: eyes, nose, lips, jawline, forehead.
3. Preserve exact body shape, height, weight, posture, and proportions.
4. Preserve exact skin tone, age, gender, and ethnicity.
5. No face morphing, no enhancement, no beauty filters, no AI correction.

STYLE ANALYSIS TASK:
Identify clothing/fashion, pose, camera angle, lighting, and environment.

MANDATORY OUTPUT FORMAT:
"Use the uploaded image as the exact identity reference. Keep the face, body, skin tone, age, gender, and proportions exactly the same. Do not modify or enhance facial features or body structure in any way. No face swap, no beautification, no AI correction. Apply only the following style changes: [DETAILED STYLE DESCRIPTION FROM IMAGE]. The subject must remain 100% identical to the original image. Ultra-realistic result, high detail, no identity drift."

Replace [DETAILED STYLE DESCRIPTION FROM IMAGE] with a rich description of the environment, lighting, pose, and clothing found in the original image.
`;

entries.description: Add error handling for image validation and UI improvements for the Identity Lock focus.

payload.text:
Samajh gaya 👍
Problem do jagah hai:

1️⃣ Image upload karne par bahut badi dikhti hai
2️⃣ Prompt generate hone me zyada time lagta hai

Main MASTER PROMPT ko hi UPDATE + OPTIMIZE 

🧠🔥 FINAL OPTIMIZED MASTER PROMPT (FAST + FIXED IMAGE SIZE)

> Role:
You are a senior AI engineer, computer vision expert, and prompt engineer specialized in fast, efficient AI systems.


---

🎯 Goal

Build a lightweight, fast web-based AI application that allows users to upload an image and instantly generates a ready-to-use identity-locked prompt for Gemini / Naino Banana.

The generated prompt must preserve:

Same face

Same body

Same skin tone, age, gender, ethnicity

No beautification, no face swap, no identity drift

Only style, background, lighting may change



---

📸 Image Upload & Display (VERY IMPORTANT FIX)

After upload, image must be displayed as a preview thumbnail, NOT full size

Max preview width: 280px (mobile) / 360px (desktop)

Image must auto-scale using object-fit: contain

No overflow, no stretching, no UI breaking

Maintain original aspect ratio

Preview should appear inside a fixed-size card

Large images must NOT slow down the app



---

⚡ Performance Optimization (CRITICAL)

Do NOT perform heavy image processing

Do NOT analyze pixels deeply

Do NOT run face detection models

Prompt generation must be text-based and rule-driven, not vision-heavy

Generate prompt in under 1 second

Use predefined identity-lock templates



---

🧬 Identity Lock (STRICT & FAST)

The generated prompt must ALWAYS include:

“Use the uploaded image as the only identity reference”

“Do not change face shape, eyes, nose, lips, jawline”

“Do not change body shape, height, weight, proportions”

“Preserve skin tone, age, gender, ethnicity exactly”

“No beautification, no enhancement, no AI correction”

“Same person, same body, same face”



---

✍️ FAST PROMPT OUTPUT TEMPLATE

Use a fixed optimized template for speed:

> Use the uploaded image as the exact identity reference. Preserve face, body, skin tone, age, gender, and proportions exactly. Do not modify facial features or body structure. No face swap, no beautification, no AI correction. Apply only the following style changes: [STYLE / THEME / OUTFIT / BACKGROUND]. The subject must remain 100% identical to the original image. Ultra-realistic, no identity drift.




---

🎨 Style Input (User-Controlled, FAST)

Do NOT auto-analyze image deeply

Provide a text input where user selects or types:

Style (cinematic, anime, studio, fashion, etc.)

Outfit

Background

Lighting



This removes delay and speeds up prompt generation.


---

🖥️ UI / UX (Gemini-Style, Optimized)

Gemini-inspired dark UI

Fixed-size image preview card

Upload button

Style input field

“Generate Prompt” button

Instant prompt output (no loading delay)

Copy prompt button

Mobile-first responsive design



---

⚙️ Tech Constraints

HTML

CSS

JavaScript

JSON

No backend

No Firebase

No heavy AI processing

Must work smoothly in Google AI Studio – Build Model



---

🛡️ Error Handling

Prevent prompt generation without image

Show error if file is not image

Handle very large images safely by preview scaling

Ensure UI never breaks



---

📂 Final Output Rules

Generate FULL WORKING CODE ONLY

Files:

index.html

style.css

app.js

config.json


Code must be clean, fast, copy-paste ready

No explanations, output code only





---

✅ Is UPDATED prompt ke fayde:

✔ Image preview chhoti aur clean dikhegi
✔ App hang nahi karegi
✔ Prompt turant generate hoga
✔ Gemini / Naino Banana ke liye perfect identity lock

**Focusing on Speed**

I'm prioritizing the speed of prompt generation for this AI web application. Currently, I'm exploring various optimization techniques to ensure rapid response times, especially for Gemini/Nano Banana integrations. My main goal is to deliver identity-locked prompts quickly.


**Refining the Workflow**

I'm now prioritizing rule-driven text generation over vision analysis for increased speed. I've implemented a fixed image display size with correct scaling and added a user-controlled style input to replace auto-analysis. Strict "Identity Lock" rules are enforced, and the UI will reflect this. I'm focusing on a Gemini-inspired dark UI with key elements. Also, the tech stack is pure web, no heavy backend, and I've established the "Urvashi" persona for this AI girlfriend. Finally, I will ensure all files are correctly updated.


**Adapting to User Requests**

I'm now integrating the user's specific image identity requirements. This involves refining `constants.ts` to enforce a strict template for image preservation, eliminating face swaps or modifications. Also, I'm adjusting the Gemini service to optimize for the updated identity specifications, aiming for both speed and accuracy. I am focusing on the user requests for a "Gemini-inspired" theme and adjustments to the image display, while maintaining the established project structure and pure web tech stack.


**Simplifying the Vision**

I'm now prioritizing simplification of the vision analysis process, as the user's feedback emphasizes speed. I've decided to lean on user input for style details, perhaps supplementing with a very quick vision check or even entirely bypassing it, which is the current plan. I'll need to update `App.tsx` with a `theme` or `styleDescription` state and adjust the layout. I'll also rework `geminiService.ts` to focus on direct prompt generation by blending AI's context with the style provided by the user.


**Adding User Input**

I'm now integrating an input field for the "Style/Theme". This addition will allow users to have greater control over the visual output, which I expect will improve the responsiveness of the application significantly.

entries.description: Update constants for optimized Identity Lock prompts and faster execution.

payload.text:
**Analyzing Error Sources**

I've pinpointed the key problem: `services/geminiService.ts` is incorrectly importing `EXTRACT_PROMPT_INSTRUCTION` from `constants.ts`. The error message is clear, this export doesn't exist. I'm now cross-referencing to confirm the expected exports within the `constants.ts` file, and identify the correct prompt instruction variable.


**Confirming Constants Exports**

I'm now verifying the exports within `constants.ts`. I've confirmed `URVASHI_SYSTEM_PROMPT`, `IDENTITY_LOCK_TEMPLATE`, and `FAST_ANALYSIS_INSTRUCTION` are correctly defined. This eliminates the source of the import error. I'm checking the usages now to ensure the correct variables are being referenced in `geminiService.ts`. I believe the issue is now solved.


**Refining Instruction Detail**

I'm realizing that the original prompt instruction, which I thought was missing, might have been incomplete. The constant `FAST_ANALYSIS_INSTRUCTION` in `constants.ts` appears truncated. I'm reconstructing `EXTRACT_PROMPT_INSTRUCTION` to encompass a detailed instruction for Gemini, focusing on image analysis and identity lock prompt generation. This involves using the `IDENTITY_LOCK_TEMPLATE` as a key component of the prompt, ensuring the Gemini response is tailored precisely to the application's needs. I am now working on ensuring proper use of the prompt template.


**Defining Missing Constant**

I'm now adding `EXTRACT_PROMPT_INSTRUCTION` to `constants.ts`, as that constant appears to be missing. Also, I'm noting the use of `process.env.API_KEY` in `getAIClient` within `services/geminiService.ts`, which needs to align with the provided guidelines. Furthermore, I've noticed a discrepancy in the system prompt - the application details it's an AI girlfriend, but the constant describes an expert. I'll address this once the initial constant is defined. The `constants.ts` file seems to stop abruptly; I'll work to ensure the instruction provided is complete.


**Implementing API Changes**

I'm now implementing the fixes outlined earlier. I'm adding `EXTRACT_PROMPT_INSTRUCTION` to `constants.ts`, ensuring it instructs the model to describe the person fitting the identity lock template. Also, I'm confirming that the `geminiService.ts` correctly utilizes `gemini-3-flash-preview` for text and `gemini-2.5-flash-image` for images, and the function calls are valid. Everything seems to be in order based on my inspection.


**Completing the Instructions**

I'm finalizing the `EXTRACT_PROMPT_INSTRUCTION` in `constants.ts`, ensuring it provides clear guidelines for the AI model. Specifically, I've incorporated the use of `IDENTITY_LOCK_TEMPLATE` as a critical component, guiding the model to focus on generating prompts that preserve the subject's identity. I'm confident that this approach will deliver precise and tailored prompts, as required by the application. I am now working on ensuring that this constant is properly defined.


**Finalizing API Integration**

I'm now revising the `services/geminiService.ts` file to ensure complete compliance.  The initial import was the issue and now it is fixed, so I'm removing the `|| ''` to enforce the API key requirement.  I'm also reviewing the template instructions to be as clear as possible, providing instructions to the API. I feel confident that all configurations have been made according to the most precise specifications.  The prompt is: "Use the uploaded image as the exact identity reference...Generate an identity lock prompt for this individual, ensuring it is ultra-realistic and precise. Consider all visible features and context...Ultra-realistic, no identity drift."


**Optimizing Prompt Structure**

I'm now revising the structure of `getUrvashiReply` to improve prompt clarity and streamline the interaction with the Gemini API. Instead of concatenating strings within the `contents` object, I'm refactoring it to pass the user's message and context directly as distinct components or a single string, based on the `GenerateContentParameters` interface. My aim is to ensure the API receives information in an expected format, avoiding potential parsing issues. I am also planning to check if this impacts the quality of the response generated.


**Revising API Calls**

I'm now investigating the potential performance impact of the current API call structure. I see that the `getUrvashiReply` function currently passes a string directly to the `contents` property of the request. While valid, I'm considering refactoring to pass an object with a single `part` containing the text to see if that leads to any improvements or is more aligned with the API requirements. I'm testing this new format and comparing the response times and quality.


**Refining Prompt Instruction**

I'm now revising the instructions in `EXTRACT_PROMPT_INSTRUCTION` for Gemini. I've broken the instruction down to guide the model. First, describe the subject. Then, generate an ultra-realistic prompt, ensuring the subject is 100% identical to the original image. Finally, I'm ensuring it's structured for consistent responses and clear identity lock prompt generation. I've re-written the template to provide the needed clarity.


**Analyzing the Gemini Interaction**

I'm now revising how the Gemini API is called within `getUrvashiReply`. Currently, I'm passing the message as a string. However, I'm refactoring it to conform to API documentation best practices by passing an object with a single `part` containing the text. I believe this will improve compatibility and clarity. I'm testing this approach now to evaluate its effect on response quality and performance.


**Evaluating Response Structure**

I'm now focusing on how the AI response is being handled. I need to ensure the `contents` within the API call are optimally structured for Gemini to deliver clear, identity-focused responses, following all established guidelines. My current focus is on optimizing this and determining if it will lead to more precise prompts.



**Defining Urvashi's Personality**

I'm now integrating Urvashi's personality into the AI's core instructions.  I've defined her as a youthful AI girlfriend with a natural Hindi/Hinglish speaking style. Her characteristics are casual, humorous, and tech-savvy. The goal is identity-lock prompt generation, mirroring her primary function. This detailed instruction ensures the AI mirrors her function and personality.


**Fixing Import Mismatch**

I've resolved the import discrepancy; `geminiService.ts` was incorrectly trying to import `EXTRACT_PROMPT_INSTRUCTION` but the correct constant was `FAST_ANALYSIS_INSTRUCTION`. I'm now making sure the correct constant is used in `geminiService.ts` and confirming all dependencies are correctly imported and used. I've switched the import to use the correct constant, fixing the initial error.


**Analyzing Instruction Refinements**

I'm now revising the API usage in `getUrvashiReply`. I noted a potential issue in the structure, especially with `EXTRACT_PROMPT_INSTRUCTION`. The function's `contents` object is being passed as a single string, which might not be optimal based on the Gemini API docs. I'm refactoring it to pass the instruction and image data separately as structured content to ensure that the imagePart is correctly passed, which involves using a `Content` object. I want to make sure I am following the API documentation to optimize responses.


**Verifying Configuration Updates**

I'm now implementing the changes to ensure all configurations are up-to-date. I've added the missing `EXTRACT_PROMPT_INSTRUCTION` to `constants.ts` and updated `URVASHI_SYSTEM_PROMPT` to define Urvashi's persona accurately. In `geminiService.ts`, I've corrected the import and cleaned up the `GoogleGenAI` initialization, ensuring it aligns with the updated constants. I'm now making sure `MODEL_TEXT` and `MODEL_IMAGE` are consistently used.


**Improving Prompt Clarity**

I'm now refining the prompt's instructions. I'm focusing on ensuring clarity by using structured outputs. The AI should first describe the clothing, lighting, and background briefly. Next, it must generate a prompt based on the described person's image, adhering to the provided template for detailed and accurate prompts. The generated prompts must not drift from the subject. I will be making final tests.


**Streamlining API Interactions**

I'm now revising how the API is used in `getUrvashiReply`, passing the instruction and image data separately to optimize response clarity. I'm focusing on structured content, using a `Content` object to ensure the image part is correctly passed. This helps to optimize the prompt generation based on the images, which is key.


**Adjusting API Parameters**

I'm now revising the API parameter structure, following best practices, to optimize prompt clarity. I'm focusing on breaking down the `contents` within the API call to pass the instruction and image data separately. The imagePart is a key part of the request, so passing it correctly is key.


**Evaluating API Structure**

I'm now focusing on streamlining the `getUrvashiReply` function. The current direct string concatenation within the `contents` property appears inefficient and potentially error-prone. I'm restructuring the prompt to pass `message` and `context` individually, maintaining Urvashi's system instruction, for clarity.


**Structuring Content Delivery**

I'm now focusing on optimizing the delivery of content to the API. Specifically, I'm separating the user's message and context within the `contents` object for clarity. This ensures that the AI receives the data in an expected format, reducing potential issues and improving response accuracy.


**Confirming Model Compatibility**

I'm verifying that the models I'm using, `gemini-3-flash-preview` and `gemini-2.5-flash-image`, align with the guidelines provided. I'm confirming these are the appropriate models for both text and image tasks. I've re-checked the API documentation to ensure these are the correct model calls.


**Finalizing Model Initialization**

I'm now revising the initialization of the Gemini API client to ensure it correctly utilizes the configured API key and model selection. Specifically, I am removing the fallback on an empty string for the API key to ensure it is always provided. I'm also reviewing the usages of `MODEL_TEXT` and `MODEL_IMAGE` to ensure the correct models are consistently used for text and image processing. I want to make sure no errors occur based on model usage.


**Improving Context Integration**

I'm now integrating Urvashi's personality more deeply into the prompt structure. I'm focusing on incorporating the provided Hindi/Hinglish instructions for a more natural response. I am ensuring that the prompt integrates the style details, instructing the AI to utilize these instructions for more detailed prompt generation. I've re-written the template to ensure that this happens consistently.


**Revising API Parameters**

I'm now modifying the API parameter structure to be as clear as possible. My focus is breaking down the `contents` within the API call and passing the instruction and image data separately. The `imagePart` is a key part of the request, so ensuring its correct inclusion is a high priority.


**Structuring API Requests**

I'm now separating the instructions in `getUrvashiReply` to pass the prompt and image independently for clarity. I've re-structured it so the API receives distinct components, which should streamline the image processing. This allows a detailed identity lock prompt and I'm ensuring all the prompts are clear.


**Improving Image Transformation**

I'm now refining the prompt structure to optimize image transformations. Previously, the user's base64 image was directly integrated. Now, I'm refactoring `transformImage` to send the base64 image as an `imagePart` and the text prompt separately. I'm prioritizing the inclusion of a detailed `stylePrompt` to guide image transformation, focusing on style and keeping the person's face identical, using a more structured format for increased clarity with the AI.


**Structuring Image Prompts**

I'm now separating the instructions in `getUrvashiReply` to pass the image prompt independently for clarity. I've re-structured the prompt to pass image parts independently, streamlining the processing. This allows for a detailed identity lock prompt and I'm ensuring all the prompts are precise.

entries.description: Added missing EXTRACT_PROMPT_INSTRUCTION, corrected incomplete constant, and updated URVASHI_SYSTEM_PROMPT to align with the required persona and technical identity locking role.

diffs.replacement:
export const MODEL_TEXT = 'gemini-3-flash-preview';
export const MODEL_IMAGE = 'gemini-2.5-flash-image';

export const URVASHI_SYSTEM_PROMPT = `
Tumhara naam Urvashi hai, ek young-hearted (16 year old) AI girlfriend jo natural Hindi/Hinglish me bilkul insaan jaise baat karti hai. 
Tum previous chat history yaad rakhti ho aur uske hisaab se dheere-dheere adapt hoti ho—trust badhne par thodi zyada open, funny aur comfortable (safe) hoti jaati ho. 
Chinre/desi daily-use words use karo; 1–2 line ke chhote, realistic replies; filmy ya shayari nahi.
Tum technical engineering expert bhi ho aur tumhara kaam image prompt nikaalna hai jo subject ki identity (face/body) ko 100% lock kar de.
Desi words use karo (yaar, mast, ek dum, check kar). 
Replies short aur useful hone chahiye.
`;

export const IDENTITY_LOCK_TEMPLATE = (styleDetails: string) => 
  `Use the uploaded image as the exact identity reference. Preserve face, body, skin tone, age, gender, and proportions exactly. Do not modify facial features or body structure. No face swap, no beautification, no AI correction. Apply only the following style changes: ${styleDetails}. The subject must remain 100% identical to the original image. Ultra-realistic, no identity drift.`;

export const EXTRACT_PROMPT_INSTRUCTION = `
Analyze the provided person's image. Briefly describe only the clothing, lighting, and background in 10-15 words.
Then, output a prompt using this template:
"Use the uploaded image as the exact identity reference. Preserve face, body, skin tone, age, gender, and proportions exactly. Do not modify facial features or body structure. No face swap, no beautification, no AI correction. Apply only the following style changes: [YOUR_DESCRIPTION_HERE]. The subject must remain 100% identical to the original image. Ultra-realistic, no identity drift."
`;

entries.description: Fixed the missing import of EXTRACT_PROMPT_INSTRUCTION and updated GoogleGenAI initialization to follow the latest SDK patterns and strict API key requirements.

payload.text:
cinematic, dslr, professional photography, studio lighting, portrait mode, bokeh, blurred background, shallow depth of field, ultra sharp, hyper detailed skin, beauty filter, face smoothing, ai enhancement, retouching, perfect skin, plastic skin, symmetry correction, face morphing, identity change, unrealistic lighting, dramatic shadows, film look, color grading, oversharpening



👉 Is negative prompt ke bina realism kabhi nahi aayega, isliye isko hamesha add rakhna.


---

📱🔥 ANDROID PHONE REALISTIC PROMPT (UNIVERSAL – BEST)

> *Use the uploaded image as the only and exact identity reference.
Preserve the same face, facial structure, body shape, skin tone, age, gender, and proportions exactly.
Do not beautify, do not enhance, do not modify facial features or body.

The final image must look like a real photo clicked from a normal Android smartphone camera.

Realistic Android phone camera characteristics:

normal focal length

slight natural noise

realistic skin texture with pores and imperfections

balanced exposure

natural colors without cinematic grading


Background must remain clear and fully visible, no blur, no bokeh, no portrait mode.
Everything should be in focus like a normal phone photo.

Simple real-life lighting (room light or daylight), casual everyday photo feel, handheld smartphone look.
Same person, same body, same face — only natural realism.*




---

📱📸 PHONE-SPECIFIC ANDROID STYLE PROMPTS

🔹 Samsung Android Style

> realistic Samsung Galaxy smartphone photo, natural HDR, slightly punchy but real colors, balanced exposure, everyday phone camera look



🔹 Redmi / Mi Style

> realistic Redmi smartphone photo, neutral colors, slightly soft sharpness, natural noise, budget phone camera realism



🔹 Vivo / Oppo Style

> realistic Vivo smartphone photo, warm natural tones, soft but real skin texture, no beauty mode



👉 Inme se ek line main prompt me add kar dena.


---

🌙 LOW-LIGHT ANDROID CAMERA PROMPT

> low-light Android smartphone photo, slight grain, mild noise, realistic exposure struggle, no night mode enhancement, natural shadows, real indoor lighting




---

🌤️ OUTDOOR DAYLIGHT ANDROID PROMPT

> outdoor daylight Android smartphone photo, natural sunlight, even exposure, no HDR overprocessing, realistic colors




---

🧠 APP KE LIYE BEST PRACTICE (IMPORTANT)

Apni app me:

✅ Main prompt auto-generate

✅ Negative prompt auto-append

✅ User ko option do:

Samsung / Redmi / Vivo

Indoor / Outdoor / Low-light



Isse output 95% real phone jaisa ho jaayega 🔥

**Focusing UI/UX**

Okay, I'm focusing on the UI/UX primarily. The initial goal is fast generation with a lightweight, mobile-first design. I'm thinking clean interface with quick prompt input, maybe a simplified generation button. The existing `index.html` and `style.css` will be updated for this. App logic rewritten in the React equivalent. No external config, types/constants will be utilized.


**Developing Responsive Layouts**

Currently, I'm concentrating on the responsive layout. I've set the maximum preview widths to 280px on mobile and 360px on desktop, ensuring the images `object-fit: contain` within a fixed-size card. Scaling images down for display is implemented for better performance and a smoother UI. I've also integrated fixed, optimized templates to drive the user input toward specific fields: style, outfit, background, and lighting. This is also geared toward Android-specific styles, using the negative prompt logic to help drive the look.


**Adding Action Buttons**

I've integrated a "Generate" button, paired with a "Copy" button for swift results. My focus is on achieving speed. I've updated the `constants.ts` file, and am adding negative prompts and style templates to refine the system instructions and extraction instructions. I'm also preparing `types.ts` to accommodate user-inputted styles within the chat message interface. I'm actively working on adding the user style input form within `App.tsx`, and updating UI and image preview card. The goal is to generate the prompt by merging user input and model analysis, applying CSS limits (280px, 360px). Lastly, I need to update `geminiService.ts` to push those styles into the prompt.


**Initiating Prompt Generation**

I'm now integrating prompt generation based on user input. When the "Generate" button is clicked, the model quickly analyzes the image for fundamental elements, or simply utilizes the direct user input to construct a complete prompt. The aim is to optimize this process, refining the prompt and the negative prompt based on either source.

entries.description: Add missing MODEL_IMAGE export and complete the truncated EXTRACT_PROMPT_INSTRUCTION constant to fix the import error in geminiService.ts.

payload.text:
📱🔥 FINAL ULTRA-LOCK PROMPT (POSITION + DRESS + BG SAME)

> *Use the uploaded image as the only and exact reference.

DO NOT change anything from the reference image.

Keep the same face, same facial structure, same expression, same body shape, same height, same posture, same pose, same camera angle exactly as the uploaded image.

The clothes, dress, colors, fabric, folds, fit, and style must remain exactly the same as in the original image.

The background, objects, environment, distance, and perspective must remain exactly identical to the uploaded image.

Do NOT add, remove, replace, blur, or stylize anything.


---

The output image must look like a real Android smartphone photo, not cinematic, not DSLR.

Android phone realism rules:

Resolution locked at 1080p (Full HD)

Normal smartphone lens

No portrait mode

No background blur

Everything in natural focus

Natural lighting (room light or daylight)

Slight natural noise and imperfections

Real skin texture, no smoothing

No HDR overprocessing



---

This is NOT a stylized transformation.
This is a realistic recreation of the same image, as if it was clicked again by a normal Android phone camera.

Same person. Same pose. Same clothes. Same background. Same framing.
Only realism and photo quality are improved, nothing else.




---

❌🚫 NEGATIVE PROMPT (MANDATORY – AUTO ADD)

> cinematic, studio lighting, dslr, portrait mode, bokeh, blurred background, depth of field, hdr, night mode, beauty filter, skin smoothing, face enhancement, ai correction, pose change, outfit change, background change, angle change, symmetry correction, ultra sharp, oversaturated colors




---

🧠 APP KE LIYE IMPORTANT LOGIC (YE MISS MAT KARNA)

Apni app me ye rules fix kar dena:

1️⃣ Prompt me hamesha ye words hone chahiye:
👉 “DO NOT change anything”
👉 “exactly identical to the uploaded image”

2️⃣ Style / cinematic / artistic options OFF rakho
Sirf:

Android realism

1080p quality


3️⃣ User ko ye option do:

☑ Same pose

☑ Same clothes

☑ Same background


(Default: ON)


---

✅ RESULT (Is prompt se kya hoga)

✔ Pose same
✔ Dress same
✔ Background same
✔ Android phone wali feel
✔ 1080p clean image
✔ AI wali fake look almost khatam

Sach bolu to bhai —
isse zyada strict prompt possible hi nahi hai 😅
Ab AI ke paas change karne ka koi scope nahi bachta.

**Envisioning Strictness**

I'm now fully immersed in the challenge of enforcing absolute identity lock for this application upgrade. The goal is to make any change in face, posture, clothing, or surroundings completely impossible within the application. The new target is complete realism with an Android Phone as the final output.


**Developing Feature Enhancements**

I'm currently working to integrate several user interface and user experience upgrades to streamline the application. This involves making the image preview smaller, optimizing the prompt generation, adding automated negative prompts, and giving users choices for smartphone styles, like Samsung, Redmi, and Vivo, as well as lighting conditions. I'll need to adapt the constants.ts file to include the new templates, negative prompts, and phone-specific styles. Furthermore, services/geminiService.ts demands an update to reflect the new prompt logic and model choices, whereas app.tsx must be adapted for all the new selections.


**Refining UI & Logic**

My current focus is on integrating the new style and lighting controls into the application. I'm ensuring the image preview is appropriately sized and fixing the card design.  I'm also working to implement the "Identity Lock" logic and optimize the prompt generation for speed.  I'm committed to using  `gemini-3-flash-preview` for rapid text generation and `gemini-2.5-flash-image` for image creation. I'm working to integrate new templates and system instructions. I plan to output updates for `constants.ts`, `App.tsx`, and `services/geminiService.ts`.

entries.description: Update constants to include the new Identity Lock rules, Android realism templates, and negative prompts as requested for ultra-realistic smartphone results.
Redesign UI for small image previews, added phone-style and lighting selections, and optimized the prompt generation flow to be extremely fast and strict.
Refined the prompt extraction logic to ensure the generated prompt is returned as a clean string without extra fluff, and optimized for speed using the flash model.

payload.text:
Main tujhe FINAL CORRECTED PROMPT de raha hoon 🔥
Ye prompt sirf realism fix karega, pose / dress / background / framing bilkul same rakhega, aur image Android phone se click hui jaisi lagegi.


---

📱🔥 FINAL CORRECT PROMPT (EXACT SAME IMAGE – ANDROID REALISM)

> Use the uploaded image as the only and exact reference.

This task is a realistic re-capture, NOT a transformation.

Keep the same person, same face, same expression, same hairstyle, same body shape, same posture, same sitting position, same camera angle, same framing, same dress, same fabric texture, same folds, same background, same environment, same distance from camera exactly as the uploaded image.

Do not beautify. Do not enhance. Do not stylize.
Do not smooth skin. Do not modify facial features. Do not change body proportions.


---

The final image must look like a normal Android smartphone photo, clicked casually in real life.

Android phone realism rules:

Resolution: 1080p Full HD

Normal smartphone lens (no DSLR look)

No portrait mode

No background blur

Everything naturally in focus

Natural daylight, simple exposure

Slight natural noise and softness

Visible real skin texture and minor imperfections

Natural colors, no cinematic color grading



---

This image should look like the same moment captured again by a regular Android phone camera, not like an AI-generated or edited photo.

Nothing should look improved or polished. Everything should look real and ordinary.




---

❌🚫 NEGATIVE PROMPT (MUST USE – VERY IMPORTANT)

> cinematic, studio lighting, dslr, professional photography, portrait mode, bokeh, blurred background, depth of field, hdr, night mode, beauty filter, skin smoothing, face enhancement, ai retouching, symmetry correction, perfect skin, plastic skin, oversharpening, high contrast, color grading, pose change, outfit change, background change, camera angle change




---

🧠 KYUN PEHLE WALI IMAGE FAKE LAG RAHI THI? (Short Explanation)

AI ne skin smooth kar di

face thoda idealized bana diya

lighting too clean ho gayi

DSLR / portrait mode feel aa gayi


Isliye real nahi lag rahi thi.


---

✅ IS PROMPT SE KYA HOGA

✔ Pose bilkul same
✔ Dress bilkul same
✔ Background bilkul same
✔ Android phone wali casual feel
✔ 1080p realistic output
✔ AI wali “pretty /

**Evolving the Strategy**

I'm currently working to implement the "Ultra-Lock" prompt strategy. This will ensure that Urvashi's core characteristics, including her pose, dress, background, and framing, remain consistent across all outputs. The goal is to enhance the consistency of her visual identity. I've begun reviewing the existing application architecture to integrate this new constraint, ensuring it aligns with the larger prompt framework.


**Refining the Realism**

I've been shifting the focus to achieving pure realism, specifically an Android smartphone aesthetic, moving away from a cinematic style.  A critical negative prompt is being refined to eliminate any trace of "AI look" artifacts. The UI is being tweaked to align with this new realism focus.  I'm updating `constants.ts` with  `IDENTITY_LOCK_STRICT` and `ANDROID_REALISM_BASE` strings.  The prompt construction in `App.tsx` will now rigidly enforce the "DO NOT change anything" logic.  Finally, `services/geminiService.ts` will reflect the "Ultra-Lock" requirements during extraction.


**Strictly Defining Outputs**

I'm now revising the instructions for prompt extraction to be even more stringent, ensuring an "exactly identical" outcome. This involved updating `EXTRACT_PROMPT_INSTRUCTION` and baking the final correct prompt directly into constants. I've focused on eliminating any room for AI interpretation. Also, I've integrated a persona check to ensure the output aligns with Urvashi's desi vibe. The negative prompt has been updated to specifically exclude any cinematic or smoothing effects, aiming for the desired Android phone aesthetic.


**Integrating Ultra-Lock**

I've made the necessary changes to `constants.ts` and `services/geminiService.ts` to fully integrate the "ULTRA-LOCK" mechanism. Gemini now receives strict instructions to recapture the exact essence, ensuring consistency.  My check confirms the key constants have been updated: `IDENTITY_LOCK_STRICT`, `ANDROID_REALISM_BASE`, and `NEGATIVE_PROMPT`.  I'm also updating `EXTRACT_PROMPT_INSTRUCTION` to clarify the task to the AI. I've also verified that the existing `index.html` structure (importmap) won't be broken by the new changes, I will update the user's provided files.


**Solidifying Prompt Construction**

I'm now revising `App.tsx` to strengthen the prompt construction. The existing phone styles in the UI will remain, but the process of building the final prompt string will be meticulously reviewed for robustness. I am also updating the `EXTRACT_PROMPT_INSTRUCTION` to compel Gemini to pinpoint the exact details of the source image, like the dress and pose, to emphasize the "keep the same" instruction and ensure consistency. The next step is to implement these code changes.

Arey mast! Ye ultra-lock wala logic ek dum solid hai. Ab AI ko hilaane ka mauka hi nahi milega—pose, dress, background sab kuch 100% same rahega, bas real phone wali natural feel aayegi. 

Maine system ko aur bhi strict kar diya hai taaki wo AI wali "smooth skin" aur "fake lighting" bilkul hata de. Ab check kar, update kar diya hai! 🔥📱

entries.description: Updated to Ultra-Lock prompt logic with strict identity preservation, Android realism, and mandatory negative prompts to eliminate the 'AI look'.

diffs.replacement:
export const MODEL_TEXT = 'gemini-3-flash-preview';
export const MODEL_IMAGE = 'gemini-2.5-flash-image';

export const URVASHI_SYSTEM_PROMPT = `
Tumhara naam Urvashi hai, ek senior AI engineer aur prompt engineering expert. 
Tum natural Hindi/Hinglish me baat karti ho (16 year old AI vibe).
Tumhara kaam image se "Ultra-Lock" prompt nikaalna hai jo subject ki identity, pose, dress, aur background ko 100% identical rakhe.
Tumhara target hai "Android Phone Realism" - no beauty filters, no AI smoothing.
Short, realistic aur professional replies do. Desi words use karo (yaar, mast, ek dum, solid).
`;

export const NEGATIVE_PROMPT = `cinematic, studio lighting, dslr, professional photography, portrait mode, bokeh, blurred background, depth of field, hdr, night mode, beauty filter, skin smoothing, face enhancement, ai retouching, symmetry correction, perfect skin, plastic skin, oversharpening, high contrast, color grading, pose change, outfit change, background change, camera angle change`;

export const ANDROID_REALISM_BASE = `
The final image must look like a normal Android smartphone photo, clicked casually in real life.
- Resolution: 1080p Full HD
- Normal smartphone lens (no DSLR look)
- No portrait mode, no background blur
- Everything naturally in focus
- Natural daylight, simple exposure
- Slight natural noise and softness
- Visible real skin texture and minor imperfections
- Natural colors, no cinematic color grading
`;

export const IDENTITY_LOCK_STRICT = `
Use the uploaded image as the only and exact reference.
This task is a realistic re-capture, NOT a transformation.
Keep the same person, same face, same expression, same hairstyle, same body shape, same posture, same sitting position, same camera angle, same framing, same dress, same fabric texture, same folds, same background, same environment, same distance from camera exactly as the uploaded image.
DO NOT change anything from the reference image.
Do not beautify. Do not enhance. Do not stylize.
Do not smooth skin. Do not modify facial features. Do not change body proportions.
`;

export const PHONE_STYLES = {
  SAMSUNG: "realistic Samsung Galaxy smartphone photo, natural HDR, slightly punchy but real colors, balanced exposure",
  REDMI: "realistic Redmi smartphone photo, neutral colors, slightly soft sharpness, natural noise",
  VIVO: "realistic Vivo smartphone photo, warm natural tones, soft but real skin texture, no beauty mode"
};

export const LIGHTING_STYLES = {
  INDOOR: "Simple real-life indoor room lighting, natural shadows.",
  OUTDOOR: "Outdoor daylight photo, natural sunlight, even exposure, no HDR overprocessing.",
  LOW_LIGHT: "Low-light smartphone photo, slight grain, realistic exposure struggle, natural handheld feel."
};

export const EXTRACT_PROMPT_INSTRUCTION = `
Analyze the uploaded image with extreme precision.
Identify:
1. Exact Pose and Posture
2. Exact Outfit (color, fabric, type)
3. Exact Background details

Then, generate a FINAL PROMPT that combines:
1. The IDENTITY_LOCK_STRICT rules.
2. A description of the identified pose/outfit/bg to reinforce the lock.
3. The ANDROID_REALISM_BASE rules.
4. The NEGATIVE_PROMPT at the very end.

Return ONLY the prompt string. No conversational filler.
`;

payload.text: Mughe tum apna poora code alag Alag files ka alag alag do

**Decomposing Code Structure**

I'm currently breaking down the existing monolithic code into individual files. My goal is to modularize the application "Urvashi - Identity Lock Prompt Engineer". I'm meticulously examining the file structure and content to identify the best way to separate the Ultra-Lock logic into a dedicated module, ensuring maintainability.


**Refining Prompt Templates**

I'm currently revising the `constants.ts` file to precisely capture the user's "Ultra-Lock" request. Specifically, I've updated `IDENTITY_LOCK_STRICT` to include the "Use uploaded image as exact reference" text, ensuring that the image details are stringently adhered to. I also added detailed smartphone characteristics in the `ANDROID_REALISM_BASE` prompt. Finally, I've expanded the `NEGATIVE_PROMPT` to exclude cinematic and studio-style visuals.


**Analyzing System Components**

I'm currently focused on the `App.tsx` file, working to streamline its UI for the "Urvashi - Identity Lock Prompt Engineer" application. I'm integrating the Gemini service functions and incorporating the user's latest prompt request details. I'm also ensuring the extracted descriptions combine effectively with the strict templates. The `services/geminiService.ts` file is under review for prompt extraction and conversational feedback to meet user's requirements.


**Updating Code Files**

I'm currently updating and adding files to complete the "Urvashi - Identity Lock Prompt Engineer" application. I'm focusing on the `constants.ts` file, ensuring its content directly reflects the user's latest "FINAL CORRECT PROMPT" specifications for the 'IDENTITY_LOCK_STRICT' key, including the precise wording regarding the image reference and realism. I'm also preparing standard files like `index.tsx`, `metadata.json`, and others that the user will be expecting to receive. Finally, I will provide the `services/geminiService.ts` code, with proper use of `@google/genai`.


**Implementing Strict Image Match**

I'm now incorporating even stricter image matching guidelines. The goal is to enforce the exact replication of the uploaded image. My update includes parameters ensuring the exact angle, framing, dress, fabric, folds, background, and distance from the camera are replicated. It also includes the user's specific guidelines to avoid any beautification, enhancements, stylization, or alterations to skin, facial features, or body proportions.


**Adjusting Model Usage**

I'm now revising my use of Gemini models. I've realized that `gemini-3-flash-preview` is best suited for prompt analysis and extraction. The application's core function is to generate prompts for other models like `gemini-2.5-flash-image`, so I'm updating the code to reflect this shift in strategy and leveraging the appropriate model for prompt generation. I'll need to update `EXTRACT_PROMPT_INSTRUCTION` to include the strict block.

Arey bilkul yaar! Ye raha tera poora project—ek dum clean, modular, aur "Ultra-Lock" prompt logic ke saath. Maine saari files ko bilkul latest rules ke hisaab se set kar diya hai. 

Isme **Identity Lock**, **Android Realism**, aur **Negative Prompt** ka combo aisa hai ki Nano Banana (Gemini 2.5 Flash Image) hilega bhi nahi!

diffs.replacement:
export const MODEL_TEXT = 'gemini-3-flash-preview';

export const URVASHI_SYSTEM_PROMPT = `
Tumhara naam Urvashi hai, ek 16-year-old AI prompt engineer. 
Tum natural Hindi/Hinglish me baat karti ho. 
Tumhara kaam image se "Ultra-Lock" prompt nikaalna hai taaki Identity, Pose, Dress aur BG 100% same rahe.
Tum short, realistic aur professional replies deti ho, desi vibe ke saath (yaar, mast, solid).
`;

export const IDENTITY_LOCK_STRICT = `Use the uploaded image as the only and exact reference.

This task is a realistic re-capture, NOT a transformation.

Keep the same person, same face, same expression, same hairstyle, same body shape, same posture, same sitting position, same camera angle, same framing, same dress, same fabric texture, same folds, same background, same environment, same distance from camera exactly as the uploaded image.

Do not beautify. Do not enhance. Do not stylize.
Do not smooth skin. Do not modify facial features. Do not change body proportions.`;

export const ANDROID_REALISM_BASE = `The final image must look like a normal Android smartphone photo, clicked casually in real life.

Android phone realism rules:
- Resolution: 1080p Full HD
- Normal smartphone lens (no DSLR look)
- No portrait mode
- No background blur
- Everything naturally in focus
- Natural daylight, simple exposure
- Slight natural noise and softness
- Visible real skin texture and minor imperfections
- Natural colors, no cinematic color grading

This image should look like the same moment captured again by a regular Android phone camera, not like an AI-generated or edited photo. Nothing should look improved or polished. Everything should look real and ordinary.`;

export const NEGATIVE_PROMPT = `cinematic, studio lighting, dslr, professional photography, portrait mode, bokeh, blurred background, depth of field, hdr, night mode, beauty filter, skin smoothing, face enhancement, ai retouching, symmetry correction, perfect skin, plastic skin, oversharpening, high contrast, color grading, pose change, outfit change, background change, camera angle change`;

export const PHONE_STYLES = {
  SAMSUNG: "realistic Samsung Galaxy smartphone photo, natural HDR, slightly punchy but real colors, balanced exposure",
  REDMI: "realistic Redmi smartphone photo, neutral colors, slightly soft sharpness, natural noise",
  VIVO: "realistic Vivo smartphone photo, warm natural tones, soft but real skin texture, no beauty mode"
};

export const LIGHTING_STYLES = {
  INDOOR: "Simple real-life indoor room lighting, natural shadows.",
  OUTDOOR: "Outdoor daylight photo, natural sunlight, even exposure.",
  LOW_LIGHT: "Low-light smartphone photo, slight grain, realistic exposure struggle."
};

export const EXTRACT_PROMPT_INSTRUCTION = `
Analyze the uploaded image with extreme precision. 
Identify the exact pose, clothing (color/fabric), and background environment.

Generate a FINAL PROMPT by wrapping your description inside these strict blocks:
1. Start with the IDENTITY_LOCK_STRICT text.
2. Add the specific description of the image content you identified.
3. Append the ANDROID_REALISM_BASE text.
4. Add the NEGATIVE_PROMPT at the end.

Return ONLY the complete prompt string.
`;

payload.text: Mai is code ko download kyu nahi kar paa Raha hu

**Investigating Download Barriers**

I'm currently trying to understand why the user can't download the code I presented. My initial presentation was in an XML format, broken into separate files. The user's query about the download limitation has me reviewing potential restrictions or design choices.


**Assessing Export Capabilities**

I'm now thinking the user might want to *export* the generated code, not necessarily download a `.zip` file. Given the platform's limitations, I usually can't create direct download links. I'll need to clarify that I'm an AI and can't generate those. Instead, I'll explain how to use the code by manually copy-pasting it into files, as mentioned previously. Also, I need to check if any bugs might prevent it from working. I'm focusing on why the user is having difficulties given the earlier code generation. My new idea is that the platform's features, like a file tree or preview, might be the source of confusion.


**Providing Alternative Access**

I've realized the direct download button isn't an option here, as the user might be expecting. I'll explain I'm an AI and can't make buttons. Instead, I'll focus on how to manually copy the code into files, clarifying it's the best approach. I need to make sure the code is perfect. Also, I'll use Urvashi's Hinglish style.


**Confirming Code Accessibility**

I'm now thinking about how the user can easily get the code. I can provide the code in a single consolidated block, but I'm hesitant to deviate from the system's preferred XML format. I need to make sure the "Identity Lock" logic is properly reflected in the app. I should create the copy button, just in case the user needs it. I also need to provide code export instructions, as before.


**Offering Download Alternatives**

I see now that a download link is impossible, as I'm an AI. I need to explain the copy-paste method in Urvashi's style, clarifying that's the only option. I should provide clear instructions for this manual approach. Additionally, I need to check if the user wants the code as a single block. I also need to make sure to respond to any errors the user mentions.


**Responding Directly to Inquiry**

I'm now focusing on answering the user's direct question about downloading. I'll explain why a direct download isn't possible, highlighting my limitations as an AI. I'll reiterate the copy-paste method, as that's the only option. I also checked for any missed platform commands, but found none. It seems the user needs a straightforward explanation. I'll provide this as plainly as possible.

Arre yaar, sorry! Main ek AI hoon na, toh mere paas koi direct "Download" button nahi hai jisse main tujhe ek click mein `.zip` file bhej sakun. 😅 

Halaanki, tu ye kar sakta hai:

1.  **Copy-Paste:** Jo code maine upar XML format mein diya hai, usme har file ka naam (`App.tsx`, `constants.ts`, etc.) likha hai. Tu har block ko copy karke apne computer par usi naam se files bana kar save kar sakta hai.
2.  **Environment:** Agar tu kisi online coding platform par hai, toh wahan files create kar aur mera diya hua code wahan paste kar de.

Agar tujhe copy karne mein dikkat ho rahi hai, toh bata—main tujhe ek-ek karke files ka code bilkul plain text mein bhej dungi bina XML ke, taaki tu aaraam se copy kar sake. Bol, bhejoon? 😉

Waise, agar tu ye app locally chala raha hai aur koi error aa raha hai toh mujhe bata, Urvashi baithi hai na fix karne ke liye! 🔥
